tlos: TLO 1. Describe the fundamental principles governing the rigid-body equations of motion for aircraft.
TLO 2. Explain the differences between body-axis, stability-axis, and wind-axis reference frames in aircraft dynamics.
TLO 3. Apply the six-degree-of-freedom equations of motion to determine aircraft translational and rotational motion.
TLO 4. Characterize the effects of simplifying assumptions on the nonlinear and linearized equations of motion.
TLO 5. Compute aircraft dynamic responses using numerical methods to solve the equations of motion.
TLO 6. Discuss modern methods of interpreting equations of motion, such as uncertainty quantification and sensitivity analysis.

events.description: The rigid body equations of motion for an aircraft are derived, and then trim solutions are presented. The equations are linearized in both state space as well as transfer function methods.
The rigid body equations of motion for an aircraft are derived, and then trim solutions are presented. The equations are linearized in both state space as well as transfer function methods.
The rigid body equations of motion for an aircraft are derived, and then trim solutions are presented. The equations are linearized in both state space as well as transfer function methods.

tlos: TLO 1. Explain concepts of aircraft static and dynamic stability
TLO 2. Collect data to determine aircraft stability characteristics
TLO 3. Analyze data from flight test to determine aircraft stability characteristics
TLO 4. Compare flight test data to Mil-Std-1797B.

events.description: The concepts of static and dynamic stability are applied to aircraft. Aircraft modes of motion are introduced. Stability derivatives and their influence are presented.
The concepts of static and dynamic stability are applied to aircraft. Aircraft modes of motion are introduced. Stability derivatives and their influence are presented.
The concepts of static and dynamic stability are applied to aircraft. Aircraft modes of motion are introduced. Stability derivatives and their influence are presented.

This event introduces several Flight Test Techniques (FTTs) used in flying qualities testing and then uses those FTTs to explore how changing stability derivatives of an aircraft changes its flying qualities.
These sorties explore the "s-plane" to further explore how FTTs are used to investigate an aircraft, and to identify and qualify modes of motion.

tlos: TLO 1. Explain classical and modern flight control systems' structure and expected behavior.
TLO 2. Demonstrate how to use Bode and root locus tools to evaluate the dynamics of a feedback control system.
TLO 3. Examine control filters and compensators and their impact to the control systems response.
TLO 4. Demonstrate usage of MIL-STD-1797 to predict flying and handling qualities.
TLO 5. Interpret the characteristics of pilot-induced oscillations.

events.description: Feedback flight control systems are introduced along with motivation for transition from mechanical to automatic flight control systems.

tlos: TLO 1. Describe basic aircraft structural components.
TLO 2. Explain the structures discipline in terms of subdisciplines.
TLO 3. Develop structures test methodology.
TLO 4. Execute elevated risk structures flight test from the cockpit or the control room.
